titleEn: From novelty to necessity: Why tulip growers are betting on robotic disease selection
titleZh: 从新奇到必需：郁金香种植者为何押注机器人病害筛选
summaryZh: 本文报道了H2L Selector 180和225两款机器人，它们能识别并处理郁金香花床中的病株，已售出139台，用户平均评分4.1/5。尽管机器人病害筛选正成为主流，但种植者强调，广泛采用仍需进一步提升可靠性和性能。文章通过四位用户的经验分享，展示了该技术在园艺领域的实际应用价值与挑战。
featuredReason: 虽然聚焦于园艺（郁金香），但机器人病害筛选技术、用户反馈与可靠性改进路径，对智慧畜牧中类似自动化病害检测（如猪只健康监测、禽类疫病识别）具有直接借鉴意义。
contentFull: From novelty to necessity: Why tulip growers are betting on robotic disease selection 16-06 | Smart farming | ArticleThe H2L Selector 180 and its larger brother, the 225, are robots that recognise and treat diseased tulips in the beds. The robots have working widths of 180 and 225 cm respectively and are produced exclusively for tulip growing. H2L has now sold 139 of them.The H2L Selector has moved beyond the experimental stage. With 139 machines sold and users giving it an average score of 4.1 out of 5, robotic disease selection is becoming mainstream in tulip production. Yet growers stress that widespread adoption will depend on further improvements in reliability and performance. Future Farming asked 4 users of the H2L Selector 180 about their experiences with the robot. The conclusion: with an average score of 4.1 on a scale of 5 they are generally positiveGet full access to all stories on Future FarmingThis Premium article is exclusively available for subscribers Obtain insights from exclusive interviews Make the right purchasing decisions with our buyers guides Dive into articles covering innovative developments All the news is brought to you by experienced editors and experts from around the world GeertHekkert Matt Mcintosh René Groeneveld See subscription optionsAlready subscribed? translationZh:
H2L Selector 180及其更大的兄弟型号225，是能够识别并处理花床中患病郁金香的机器人。这两款机器人的作业宽度分别为180厘米和225厘米，专为郁金香种植而生产。H2L目前已售出139台。

H2L Selector已走出实验阶段。凭借139台的销量和用户给出的平均4.1分（满分5分）评价，机器人病害筛选正在郁金香生产中成为主流。然而，种植者强调，广泛采用将取决于可靠性和性能的进一步改进。Future Farming采访了四位H2L Selector 180的用户，了解他们对这款机器人的使用体验。结论是：在5分制的评分中，平均得分4.1，他们普遍持积极态度。
author: Lucas van de Weijer
contentHtml: The H2L Selector has moved beyond the experimental stage. With 139 machines sold and users giving it an average score of 4.1 out of 5, robotic disease selection is becoming mainstream in tulip production. Yet growers stress that widespread adoption will depend on further improvements in reliability and performance.
